Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a database schema and a temporal profile model that shows average time intervals between activities and their standard deviations We are asked to identify anomalies in the temporal profile model generate hypotheses on why these anomalies might exist and propose verification approaches using SQL queries

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are also given that the process steps are R A E P N C So we expect the path to be R to A to E to P to N to C

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Looking at the model R to A Average 1 hour STDEV 10 minutes seems reasonable R to E Average 1 day STDEV 8 hours reasonable R to P Average 25 hours STDEV 1 hour very low STDEV This is suspicious A to C Average 2 hours but note that there is no E or P in between This might indicate premature closure E to N Average 5 minutes STDEV 1 minute very quick might indicate skipping steps P to N Average 7 days STDEV 2 days very long and variable might indicate delays N to C Average 30 minutes STDEV 5 minutes reasonable

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets list them R to P Very low STDEV for a 25 hour average This suggests a very consistent time which might be artificial P to N Very long average and high STDEV indicating inconsistent and long delays A to C Average 2 hours which is quick and note that there is no E or P in between This might indicate that claims are closed immediately after assignment without going through evaluation and approval E to N Very quick with low STDEV This might indicate that evaluation and notification are done almost instantly which might be unrealistic

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For R to P Low STDEV might be due to an automated process that runs at fixed intervals for example a batch job that approves claims every 25 hours Alternatively it might be due to a rule that sets the approval date to exactly 25 hours after receipt regardless of actual evaluation

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For P to N The long and variable delay might be due to manual notification process or due to a backlog in sending notifications It might also be due to different channels email and mail with different delays

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For A to C The quick closure might be due to auto closure rules for claims that are assigned but not processed within a certain time or due to adjuster shortcuts closing without evaluation

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For E to N The quick notification might be due to an automated notification system that triggers immediately after evaluation or due to a policy to notify customers immediately upon evaluation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are to compute the time between activities for each claim

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For R to P Average equals 90000 seconds and STDEV equals 3600 seconds So we can look for claims where the time between R and P is not within 2 hours of 25 hours That would be between 23 and 27 hours We want to find those outside

Name: Validation - PE
Text: SELECT c claim_id c claim_type ce_r timestamp as r_timestamp ce_p timestamp as p_timestamp EXTRACT EPOCH FROM ce_p timestamp minus ce_r timestamp as time_diff_seconds FROM claims c JOIN claim_events ce_r ON c claim_id equals ce_r claim_id AND ce_r activity equals R JOIN claim_events ce_p ON c claim_id equals ce_p claim_id AND ce_p activity equals P WHERE ABS EXTRACT EPOCH FROM ce_p timestamp minus ce_r timestamp minus 90000 greater than 7200

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: We can also break down by claim type adjuster and other tables by joining with adjusters and other tables However note we are to correlate with adjusters We do not have adjuster_id in claim_events The resource column might be the adjuster_id

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Lets assume resource is the adjuster_id or we can cast to integer

Name: Validation - PE
Text: SELECT c claim_id c claim_type ce_r timestamp as r_timestamp ce_p timestamp as p_timestamp EXTRACT EPOCH FROM ce_p timestamp minus ce_r timestamp as time_diff_seconds ce_a resource as adjuster_id FROM claims c JOIN claim_events ce_r ON c claim_id equals ce_r claim_id AND ce_r activity equals R JOIN claim_events ce_p ON c claim_id equals ce_p claim_id AND ce_p activity equals P JOIN claim_events ce_a ON c claim_id equals ce_a claim_id AND ce_a activity equals A WHERE ABS EXTRACT EPOCH FROM ce_p timestamp minus ce_r timestamp minus 90000 greater than 7200

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For P to N Average 7 days and STDEV 2 days We can look for claims where the time between P and N is extremely long for example more than 20 days or extremely short for example less than 1 day

Name: Validation - PE
Text: SELECT c claim_id c claim_type ce_p timestamp as p_timestamp ce_n timestamp as n_timestamp EXTRACT EPOCH FROM ce_n timestamp minus ce_p timestamp as time_diff_seconds FROM claims c JOIN claim_events ce_p ON c claim_id equals ce_p claim_id AND ce_p activity equals P JOIN claim_events ce_n ON c claim_id equals ce_n claim_id AND ce_n activity equals N WHERE EXTRACT EPOCH FROM ce_n timestamp minus ce_p timestamp greater than 1728000 OR EXTRACT EPOCH FROM ce_n timestamp minus ce_p timestamp less than 86400

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For A to C We are to check for claims that are closed immediately after assignment without going through evaluation and approval We can look for claims where the time between A and C is less than 30 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: SELECT c claim_id c claim_type ce_a timestamp as a_timestamp ce_c timestamp as c_timestamp EXTRACT EPOCH FROM ce_c timestamp minus ce_a timestamp as time_diff_seconds FROM claims c JOIN claim_events ce_a ON c claim_id equals ce_a claim_id AND ce_a activity equals A JOIN claim_events ce_c ON c claim_id equals ce_c claim_id AND ce_c activity equals C WHERE EXTRACT EPOCH FROM ce_c timestamp minus ce_a timestamp less than 1800

Name: Validation - PE
Text: To check if there is no E event between A and C SELECT c claim_id ce_a timestamp as a_timestamp ce_c timestamp as c_timestamp EXTRACT EPOCH FROM ce_c timestamp minus ce_a timestamp as time_diff_seconds FROM claims c JOIN claim_events ce_a ON c claim_id equals ce_a claim_id AND ce_a activity equals A JOIN claim_events ce_c ON c claim_id equals ce_c claim_id AND ce_c activity equals C WHERE NOT EXISTS SELECT 1 FROM claim_events ce_e WHERE ce_e claim_id equals c claim_id AND ce_e activity equals E AND ce_e timestamp greater than ce_a timestamp AND ce_e timestamp less than ce_c timestamp AND EXTRACT EPOCH FROM ce_c timestamp minus ce_a timestamp less than 1800

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For E to N Average 5 minutes and STDEV 1 minute We can look for claims where the time is less than 3 minutes or more than 7 minutes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: SELECT c claim_id c claim_type ce_e timestamp as e_timestamp ce_n timestamp as n_timestamp EXTRACT EPOCH FROM ce_n timestamp minus ce_e timestamp as time_diff_seconds FROM claims c JOIN claim_events ce_e ON c claim_id equals ce_e claim_id AND ce_e activity equals E JOIN claim_events ce_n ON c claim_id equals ce_n claim_id AND ce_n activity equals N WHERE EXTRACT EPOCH FROM ce_n timestamp minus ce_e timestamp less than 180 OR EXTRACT EPOCH FROM ce_n timestamp minus ce_e timestamp greater than 420
